metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Differentiate the proof commands for AC5 and AC6, e.g., by giving AC6's existing tests a distinct marker or naming convention (e.g., `-k 'resolve_session and not claude_code'`) so each AC is independently verifiable.
findings.finding: AC5 and AC6 share the identical proof command (`pytest tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_completion_gate.py -k resolve_session -q`), making it impossible to distinguish which test cases satisfy AC5 (the CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID capture bug fix) versus AC6 (existing `_resolve_session` tests updated and green). If the -k filter matches the same cases for both, one of the ACs is redundant; if they target different cases, the shared command cannot prove both independently.
findings.location: AC5 proof line; AC6 proof line

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `CLAUDE.md` (line 217) to the Scope IN list and update the session-fingerprint precedence description there to match the new unified order (`REBAR_SESSION_ID` → `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` → `SESSION_ID`).
findings.finding: `CLAUDE.md` line 217 documents session-fingerprint precedence as `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` first (then `REBAR_SESSION_ID / SESSION_ID`), which directly contradicts the new unified order the plan introduces (`REBAR_SESSION_ID` first). The plan lists no update to `CLAUDE.md`, leaving developer-facing documentation inconsistent with the implemented contract after the refactor.
findings.location: What section (precedence inversion) / Scope (IN list) — CLAUDE.md not mentioned anywhere in the plan
findings.suggested_fix: In CLAUDE.md line 217-218, change 'taken from `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` (then `REBAR_SESSION_ID` / `SESSION_ID`)' to 'taken from `REBAR_SESSION_ID` (then `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` / `SESSION_ID`)' and add a parenthetical noting the inversion (e.g. 'REBAR_SESSION_ID is the explicit rebar-owned override; see docs/config.md for full precedence').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC4's source-guard test to match ALL env-lookup forms (`os.getenv(`, `os.environ[`, and `os.environ.get(` with both quote styles) or, preferably, use an AST-based check (e.g. `ast.parse` + walk for `os.getenv` / `os.environ.__getitem__` / `os.environ.get` calls whose first arg is one of the three session-var names) so the guard is exhaustive regardless of coding style.
findings.finding: AC4's source-guard test checks only the literal string `os.environ.get("CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID"` (and analogous `.get(` forms) to confirm old chains are removed, but if the existing code currently uses `os.getenv("REBAR_SESSION_ID")`, `os.environ["SESSION_ID"]`, or single-quoted variants, the guard silently passes while the old env-lookup chains remain — a verify-command defect (fixture invalidity / substring false-positive).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC4 — source-guard test description

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out path (e.g., a feature flag, a temporary shim that logs a deprecation warning, or a documented git-revert procedure with the specific files to touch) for the `session_log` precedence inversion, or justify why the 'rare dual-var' scenario makes a rollback mechanism unnecessary.
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges a behavioral breaking change (precedence inversion in session_log: CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID previously won over REBAR_SESSION_ID; now REBAR_SESSION_ID wins) and explicitly removes both old resolver chains, but provides no rollback/back-out plan or expand-contract sequence. T4 requires a reversibility mechanism, not merely acknowledgement plus a low-impact claim.
findings.location: What section — 'This intentionally inverts session_log's current precedence' bullet; Scope — 'old duplicate env-lookup chains are REMOVED'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-paragraph 'Rollback' subsection to the plan: e.g., 'If the precedence inversion causes regressions, revert by swapping the first two entries in `_SESSION_ID_VARS` back to `["CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID", "REBAR_SESSION_ID", "SESSION_ID"]` in `session_id.py` and re-running CI. No schema or data migration is needed — the change is purely in-process env-var resolution.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge (in the plan or a brief inline note) why a shared module is warranted with only 2 current call-sites — e.g., cite correctness/single-source-of-truth as the primary driver, not DRY — so the Rule-of-Three exception is explicit and reviewers don't flag it as premature abstraction.
findings.finding: The new `session_id.py` abstraction has only 2 current real call-sites (`session_log._resolve_session_fp` and `transition_close._resolve_session`), which falls short of the ≥3 call-sites threshold the rubric uses to justify a new shared abstraction. The plan defers a third consumer (S5 / c557) to a future story, meaning the Rule-of-Three is satisfied only hypothetically at ship time.
findings.location: What section — 'Introduce one shared `resolve_session_id()` helper'; Scope section; Depends-on section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence in the 'Why' or 'What' section: 'Although only two call-sites exist today, the shared module is motivated by correctness (eliminating divergent resolver logic that caused the FORCE_CLOSE bug) rather than by DRY alone; the Rule-of-Three is satisfied by the correctness contract, not by call-site count.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the `_SESSION_ID_VARS` list form is justified now (vs. a plain `or`-chain) given S5 is explicitly deferred; if kept, document the iteration semantics inline to prevent a subtle gap vs. the existing `or`-chain pattern.
findings.finding: The `_SESSION_ID_VARS` list abstraction (a module-level list iterated by `resolve_session_id()`) is motivated in the plan primarily by the deferred S5 story (`privy-eloquent-nauplius`/c557), which is explicitly OUT of this story's scope. At S1 ship time there are exactly 2 call sites, both confirmed by grep (`session_log.py:57-59`, `transition_close.py:406-407`). A plain three-var `or`-chain inside `resolve_session_id()` satisfies every S1 AC identically and is trivially swapped for a list when S5 lands — the 'one-line add' property is equally true of both forms.
findings.location: What section — `_SESSION_ID_VARS = ["REBAR_SESSION_ID", "CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID", "SESSION_ID"]` definition; Scope section — 'Deferred to S5 (c557)'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) implement `resolve_session_id()` as a plain `or`-chain (`return os.environ.get('REBAR_SESSION_ID') or os.environ.get('CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID') or os.environ.get('SESSION_ID') or None`, stripping whitespace before the `or` for empty-string handling), and add `_SESSION_ID_VARS` only when S5 lands; or (b) keep the list but add an inline comment explaining the iteration pattern covers empty-string semantics identically to the `or`-chain.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
